name: authority_cleanup_contracts
outcome_goal: Establish DB-owned authority contracts for verifier refs, compiler capability routing, and roadmap registry references so runtime execution no longer depends on fabricated command strings or hardcoded route maps.
anti_requirements: Do not preserve raw shell verification as canonical runtime behavior.
Do not add new runtime fallbacks that fabricate authority.
Do not widen this packet into provider or discovery cleanup.

jobs.label: contracts_authority_packet
jobs.outcome_goal: Add the DB-backed verification and compiler contract surfaces.
jobs.anti_vibe_rule: One contract seam only; no stealth implementation of downstream packets.
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE:
Add the contract layer that makes verifier execution and compiler capability routing DB-owned.

TASK:
Introduce the canonical DB-backed contract for `verify_refs`, `verification_registry`, and compiler capability/route-hint authority. Update the emitting and consuming runtime surfaces so new producers emit `verify_refs`, legacy `verify_commands` remain read-compatible only, and missing authority rows fail explicitly.

CONSTRAINTS:
Use typed argv execution with `shell=False`. Do not leak provider-default or integration cleanup into this packet. The roadmap registry-path schema is optional metadata, not a new source of behavior.

OUTPUT_CONTRACT:
Ship the migration(s) and runtime changes required to admit verifier refs and compiler capability authority, plus any test coverage required to prove the contract.

ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA:
- New runtime producers can emit `verify_refs`.
- Consumers resolve verifier refs through DB authority only.
- Compiler capability and route-hint selection no longer depend on hardcoded maps.
- Missing authority rows fail clearly.

STOP_BOUNDARY:
Do not cut over provider defaults, discovery surfaces, or integration metadata in this packet.
